---
name: 13.20-create-20-reusable-call-workflow
description: Create the 20-reusable-call GitHub Actions workshop workflow from a natural-language specification that introduces calling a local reusable workflow.
---

# Create 20 Reusable Call Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/20-reusable-call-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate calling a reusable workflow from the same repository.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `20-reusable-call-workflow` in `.github/workflows/20-reusable-call-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce local reusable workflow invocation:

- Set a dynamic run name that includes `20-reusable-call-workflow` and the Git ref name
- Trigger on any push
- Support manual execution from the GitHub Actions UI
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Define one job named `call-reusable-workflow` that calls the local reusable workflow at `./.github/workflows/19-workflow-call-workflow.yml`
- Pass the input `report_title` with the value `Called from 20-reusable-call-workflow`
- Do not add steps directly under the calling job because reusable workflows are called at the job-level boundary

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow name, dynamic run name, trigger behavior, read-only permission, job name, local reusable workflow path, input name, and input value.
3. Keep this workflow focused on calling a reusable workflow; do not add direct job steps, secrets, artifacts, caches, service containers, or deployment behavior.
4. Use the local workflow call syntax for workflows in the same repository.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/20-reusable-call-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/20-reusable-call-workflow.yml
}
```
